_README: ILL WILL — the single home for every player-facing line of PROSE (Executor
speeches, estate instructions, game-intro guides, ceremony readings).
Edit any line here and it changes in-game — no .gd file to touch. Values are
a String or an array of strings. Arrays are either paragraphs (shown one per
screen by the Nintendo pager) or pools (one drawn at random). KEEP the
placeholders ({name}, %s, %d) and their ORDER exactly — the game fills them in
at the call site; dropping one will make that line render wrong. Symbols like
¢ (pennies) and ⚘ (wreath) are literal text. Loaded by core/dialog.gd (autoload
Dialog); call Dialog.reload() after editing to see changes live.

maskedball_coroner.open: Four turns of the knife. Everybody carries it once.
maskedball_coroner.mockery: The guests finish. Kindly laugh where it can hear you.
maskedball_coroner.none: The knife went hungry. Third is the kindest sentence.
maskedball_coroner.settle: Four knives returned. Count the pennies.
maskedball_coroner.highlight_wrong: %s wore the red wax in round %d.

greeting: Good evening. You are all here for the reading, whether you know it or not.
Welcome to the procession. Please keep your grudges inside the carriage at all times.
The estate is open. The estate is always open. The estate is never glad to see you.
Four mourners, one manor, no witnesses. Let us begin.
offering: %s leaves an offering and is repaid threefold.
The road provides for %s. The road will remember. Will you?
%s stoops for the offering. Gratitude is mandantory.
An offering for %s. The estate has logged the generosity.
%s is favoured by the wayside. Favour is a loan.
grave: %s weeps at the grave. The grave has wept harder for less.
The weeping grave takes its due from %s.
%s pays the ground what the ground is owed. Everyone will.
A grave for %s. Not theirs. Not yet.
grave_toll: %s weeps on %s's monument — and %s collects the tears (%d¢).
The grave belongs to %s. %s learns this the way everyone does: by paying.
%s bleeds onto %s's stone; %s keeps the difference (%d¢), and the grudge.
grave_goods: %s pries open the grave goods. The previous owner files no complaint.
A box for %s. Whatever is inside was buried for a reason.
%s inherits a box of goods, unexamined. Bold.
The box opens for %s. Its contents are described as 'previously loved'.
cart: %s reaches the Peddler's Cart. The Peddler stocks grievances in every size.
The cart creaks open for %s. All sales are final; so are most of the goods.
%s browses the hearse-drawn cart. Nothing here was acquired honestly.
The Peddler receives %s with the warmth of a gravestone.
seance: The planchette moves. Nobody admits to pushing it.
A séance opens. The dead are unhelpfully opinionated.
The circle turns for the whole table. Misery, at last, distributed fairly.
The medium speaks. The estate transcribes. Everyone must pay attention or pay later.
ferry: %s meets the Ferryman. The crossing is short. The bill is not.
The Ferryman takes his due from %s. He takes it from everyone.
%s pays the toll. The Ferryman does not make change.
%s pays. The river keeps the receipts.
crossroads_land: %s halts at the crossroads. The signpost offers three regrets to choose from.
The roads wait for %s. All of them lead to the gate. None of them are easy.
%s stands where the ways part. The estate recommends turning back.
arrival: %s passes the MANOR GATE. The house takes them in, which should worry them.
The gate opens for %s. Home, of a kind, and beyond the reach of grudges.
%s arrives. The Executor marks the hour in the ledger.
The manor receives %s. The procession continues without them, poorer for it.
vendetta: %s and %s settle it the estate's way — quietly, and for money.
A vendetta ripens between %s and %s. The higher stake walks away heavier.
%s stares at %s across five spaces. Sealed bids. Old wounds. New debts.
%s and %s are found within five stones of an old debt. The estate calls the wager.
vendetta_result: %s out-stakes %s and takes the difference. Grudges compound.
%s wins the wager over %s. The estate admires a decisive cruelty.
The vendetta breaks %s's way. %s adds it to the list they keep.
%s collects from %s, with interest the estate did not trouble to name.
blank: %s lands on nothing. A merciful administrative error.
%s finds bare stone. The estate resents the missed opportunity.
%s stands on plain flagstone. The estate has nothing to bill.
Bare stone for %s. Even the grudges take the night off.
%s lands nowhere in particular. The ledger notes the absence.
house_awakens: THE HOUSE AWAKENS. The manor's shadow walks the drive. Reach a safe stone or fall behind.
Something in the house remembers you. Run for the marked stones.
The shadow sweeps the procession. It is not particular about whom it catches.
will_open: The estate has reviewed the evening's conduct and finds it actionable.
The will is read. It was written some time ago, about all of you.
The estate has audited the evening and finds no one blameless.
The reading begins. The estate has weighed the night and found it wanting.
round_opener: The mourners take their marks; the estate takes notes.
The wake resumes. No one has yet asked to leave early.
The estate turns a page it wrote in advance.
The manor settles in to watch you spend.
The drive is patient; the grudges, less so.
The dead keep excellent time, and worse company.
pause: The estate pauses. The estate has nowhere in particular to be.
The proceedings are suspended at your request. The estate files the interruption.
The estate holds. It has held longer, for less.
A recess is noted. The dead do not observe recesses.
The night is paused. Nothing is resolved; nothing ever is.
The estate sets down its pen. It does not lose its place.
You have stopped the clock. The estate keeps a second clock, for occasions like these.
The record is held open. The estate has already read ahead.
idle: The estate notes %s has not moved in some time. It is used to waiting. It has never once been kept waiting so thoroughly.
%s has yet to act. The estate marks the delay in the margin.
The estate awaits %s. Its patience is a matter of record.
Nothing has been decided by %s. The estate finds this consistent with the evening so far.
%s deliberates. The estate admires deliberation, up to a point.
The estate holds a place for %s. The place is not going anywhere, and apparently neither is the night.
%s takes their time. The estate has time to spare.
quit_confirm: Departures are processed in the order received. The estate does not take them personally. It writes them down.
You are leaving. The estate has a form for this. The estate has a form for everything.
A guest withdraws. The estate marks the seat vacant and the grudges outstanding.
The exit is noted. The estate keeps the door for those who return, and a longer file for those who do not.
You wish to go. The estate will not stop you. The estate has never successfully stopped anyone.
Withdrawal acknowledged. The estate settles your account to zero and rounds the sentiment down.
One less at the table. The estate redistributes nothing, which is its custom.
The night releases you. The estate does not. The estate merely lets you believe it has.

opener: We are gathered to read aloud what the night had already decided.
The wake is spent. The estate will now account for it, name by name.
Sit. The reading is brief, the verdicts briefer, and the estate has never once been kept waiting.
The night is closed. What follows is not eulogy so much as itemisation.
Attend. The estate has balanced the evening, and finds that grief was the only currency spent freely.
closer: The will is written. It was always written. Take your grief to the door; the estate keeps the rest.
So concludes the reckoning. The estate thanks you for your custom, and remembers all of it, however politely it is asked.
That is the night. The manor will see you again; it always does.
The reading rests. Your debts are noted, your kindnesses doubted, and the door is, as ever, that way.
The estate closes the ledger. Nothing is forgiven, but everything is filed, which the estate finds nearly the same.
heir: We commend %s, who bought %s with other people's grief and now owns a house that owns them back.
%s took %s tonight and called it grief management. The estate calls it acquisition.
To %s, who leaves with %s and the room's resentment — an heir the estate can, at last, do business with.
betrayed: We remember %s, who bled %d pennies into other hands and called it Tuesday.
%s paid %d pennies to graves and gates tonight, without recourse. The estate rounds the sincerity down.
%s gave %d pennies to the ground and the tolls. Generosity is only ever involuntary here.
bloody: %s settled matters %s tonight, quietly and for money. The estate approved the paperwork retroactively.
To %s, who won %s and lost the room. The estate keeps the receipts.
hoarder: %s ends the night pennies richer and not one wreath the wiser. The estate admires a full purse and an empty claim.
%s hoarded pennies and spent it on nothing. The manor respects the discipline; the will does not reward it.
mourner: %s wept at %s tonight, expensively. Grief, the estate observes, is the one thing here that pays no dividend.
%s knelt at %s and asked the dead for nothing they could give. The estate found the manners unusual.
pious: %s knelt at %s and was rewarded under protest. Piety, the estate notes, is a poor annuity.
%s took the shrines' small mercies %s over. The saints have never met them, and intend to keep it that way.
idle: %s walked the drive and troubled no one, which the estate finds either saintly or suspicious.
%s spent the night largely unbilled. The estate resents the missed opportunity.
warm: And %s — who took from no one all night, and wept at %s besides — was in the estate's estimation, almost kind.  How unusual.
One kindness is recorded: %s harmed no one tonight, and paid at %s for the privilege. The estate has filed it under mercy.
eulogy.chronicle_prefix: The estate adds, for the record:

goals.par: Sabotage golf. Draft a trap, place it on the SHARED hole, then putt it yourself. Your trap's kills pay YOU royalties. Last round is CHAOS: everyone putts at once.
goals.echo: Duel beside your own GHOST — it replays your previous round. Shatter the others before the past catches up.
goals.tilt: The floor is one platter and everyone's weight tilts it. Fall off and you return as a vengeful seagull.
goals.orbital: Dodgeball on three tiny planets. Throws ORBIT forever — a 45-second-old ball still kills, and its thrower still gets paid.
goals.mower: Mow more lawn than anyone. Coverage is score; ramming is diplomacy.
goals.greed: One pot of gold, four sets of hands. Bank it down your chute; tackle whoever is richer.
goals.swap: Kart race where your weapon TRADES PLACES with whoever it hits. The lead is a rumor.
goals.deadweight: Sumo where the dead never leave — they possess the furniture and fling it at the living.
goals.throne: One throne, four claimants — and a throne that won't sit still. Reigning scores. Decrees blast, guards defend, and the estate heaves the seat across the hall when it tires of you.
goals.lastwill: A funeral procession race: first to the crypt inherits. Every death freezes the world while the deceased writes a curse into the road.
goals.widowsgaze: Rob the wake. Creep the parlor for relics while the Widow weeps — FREEZE when she turns, or her gaze flings you back to the rope. A shove as the sting plays is a murder.
goals.seance: A co-op séance: guide the planchette to the spirit's word — but one of you was paid in pennies to make it fail without getting caught. The Executor is the medium.
goals.understudy: Everyone knows tonight's play but the understudy, who must bluff along. Rehearse, interrogate, vote — the scoring never stalemates.
goals.maskedball: Four rotating rounds: the public Coroner's letter-opener hunts three hidden guests running icon errands, settled by one close-range accusation.
goals.pallbearers: The anthology's first TEAM race: 2v2, one coffin per pair. The coffin moves on the BLEND of both your sticks — sync to sprint, pull apart and the dead spill out. First pall to the crypt wins.

maskedball_coroner.name: THE CORONER
maskedball_coroner.goal: One visible knife hunts three hidden guests while icon errands pay pennies.
tips: Guests follow the clock, bowl, and west-hall icons. Dancers fake them too.
The public Coroner walks close and presses the action once.
A wrong accusation wears red wax and takes fourth. Silence takes third.

seance.name: THE SÉANCE
seance.goal: Guide the planchette to the word together. One of you was paid to make it fail.
tips: Whoever's paid up front sees the word too — sabotage has to look like an honest mistake.
Every hand pulls the same shared planchette. Nobody can see whose is pulling it wrong.

orbital.name: ORBITAL DODGEBALL
orbital.goal: Dodgeball on three tiny planets. Every throw orbits forever and can still kill you.
tips: A throw never truly leaves — it just keeps orbiting the cluster until physics or a body stops it.
Hop near the gap between two planets to jump to the next one.

echo.name: ECHO CHAMBER
echo.goal: Fight beside your own ghost — your past round replays and still earns you points when it lands a hit.
tips: Every translucent fighter is somebody's own past round, replaying blow for blow — yours included.
…and riposte.

deadweight.name: DEAD WEIGHT
deadweight.goal: Sumo brawl in the attic. Shove rivals off — the fallen return as furniture-hurling ghosts.
tips: A HOP dodges a shove and repositions.
A slow, telegraphed radial blast.
Hold HOP to BRACE: you resist knockback, but you're briefly exposed right after you let go.
Double-tap a direction to DASH — a quick burst to dodge or reposition.
Fall off and you possess the furniture — hurl it at the living.
Last body standing takes the round.

greed.name: GREED INC.
greed.goal: The pot fills forever. Bank it at your chute — but carrying makes you a slow, glowing target.
tips: Carry the pot to YOUR chute and hold to bank the coins as points.
The longer you hold it, the more it leaks — and the bigger a target you are.
Tackle a carrier to knock the pot loose, then scoop the spill.

throne.name: THE THRONE
throne.goal: Whoever SITS scores every second but can't move. Gang up, drain their GRIP, fling them off — and the throne itself won't hold still.
tips: On the throne: DECREE blasts the room and SUMMON a guard — but you're rooted.
Off the throne: dash in, shove the king off, and take the seat.
THE ESTATE STIRS: the throne heaves to a new spot. It glows before it goes — beat everyone there.
Longest total reign wins, not whoever sits last.

lastwill.name: LAST WILL
lastwill.goal: A funeral race where DYING IS A POWER. Three lives each — first body to the crypt inherits.
tips: Spend a life as a curse or a boulder to wreck the leaders, then respawn ahead.
HOP the traps: pendulums, spinners, and the gust gates.
First through the crypt door takes the estate.

understudy.name: THE UNDERSTUDY
understudy.goal: Three know tonight's PLAY. One is faking. Spot the Understudy — or bluff through as one.
tips: On your cue, act out a beat of the play — vague enough to hide if you're faking.
Watch who over- or under-commits; the Understudy is only guessing.
Aim with the stick, lock your vote in with A.

widowsgaze.name: THE WIDOW'S GAZE
widowsgaze.goal: Rob the wake while she weeps. FREEZE when she turns — or be taken.
tips: Hold A by a relic to lift it, carry it home, press A at your chest to bank.
SHOVE a rival as the sting rises and the Widow does your murder for you.
Once she grows suspicious, a FALLING third note is a fake-out — hold.

pallbearers.name: PALLBEARERS
pallbearers.goal: Carry the pall to the crypt first. You share one coffin — steer together or drop the dead.
tips: The coffin moves on the BLEND of both sticks. Pull the same way to sprint.
Both hop on the same beat to HEAVE over mud. Hop alone and you jostle the dead.
Drop on the downhill and the coffin bolts for the crypt without you. Chase it.

settings.hint:
Changes apply to the next game launched (estate menus apply live).
Gamepads must press a button once to be detected by the OS.

walkabout.stroll_banner: walk to a landmark  ·  A enter  ·  B desk
walkabout.idle: WALK THE GROUNDS — approach a landmark, A to enter, B back to the desk
walkabout.freeroam_header: THE GROUNDS — gather at the lychgate
walkabout.freeroam_hint: Walk the grounds (B closes this desk) · visit the wardrobe or theater · gather at the lychgate when ready

title.footer: PLAY = tonight's rite — THE PROCESSION: one wake, one board, one heir

play.header: PLAY — how does the estate settle its debts tonight?
play.procession_desc: One wake, one board. Roll THE LAST BREATH from the lychgate to the Manor Gate through three roads; minigames pay pennies and wreaths; the FINAL BELL closes each night; most wreaths INHERITS the estate.

slot.header: THE THREE ESTATES — pick where tonight happens
slot.wipe_warning: Wiping a slot erases that estate's monuments, ledger, and wardrobe. The Executor will pretend not to notice.

lobby.controls: PAD A or KEYBOARD Space/Enter takes an open seat  ·  A again = READY  ·  KB+MOUSE joins by button (its A is the mouse)  ·  ESC = players & controls

greeting.empty: The Executor is pleased to receive you. The estate had been expecting someone; it was not, in candour, you.
greeting.no_awards: Welcome back. The estate kept the lights on and the grudges filed.
greeting.seagull: The first guest left a note about the seagull. The estate declines to repeat it, but agrees it was beautiful.
pool: Welcome back. We remembered %s as %s, and see no reason to revise.
The ledger has %s down as %s. The ledger is seldom wrong twice.
%s returns. Last night they were %s. The estate expects consistency.
Do come in. %s will find their reputation as %s exactly where they left it.

album.empty: The estate has taken no portraits yet. Give it a night; it is patient, and it is watching.
album.some: %s hang in the salon. The estate remembers every face it has framed, and forgives none of them.

interim_lines: A provisional accounting, read aloud so no one may later claim surprise.
The estate pauses over the running clauses. Nothing here is final, and nothing here is forgiven.
An interim reckoning. The leaders are noted, in pencil, and watched.
The estate reads the standing so far. It has read worse, to worse people.

clauses.betrayed_desc: bled the most pennies to graves and tolls
clauses.bloody_desc: won the most on the board — vendettas and claims

narration.vendetta_alone: %s reaches the vendetta stone alone. No blood today.
narration.vendetta_wash: %s and %s stake %d each — a wash. The estate is disappointed.

interim.trailer: Provisional. The estate reserves the right to change its mind.

will_reading.unclaimed: %s — unclaimed. The estate keeps the wreath.

house_awakens.safe: Everyone reaches a safe stone. The house sulks.

heir.graffiti: %s inherited the manor at the procession

procession.epitaph_graffiti: %s had the last word over %s: “%s”. The estate filed it.

crossroads.hint: UP/DOWN TO CHOOSE · A TO COMMIT

bell.rung:
%s rings the bell at the Manor Gate.
ONE LAST TURN for everyone still on the road.
bell.closing: The road closes at dawn. The turn cap has been reached.

arrival.bumped: · the LETTERS raise it a tier

mini.error:
%s has misfiled its results.
The estate refuses to guess — no pay this cycle.

items.lucky: %s arms a LUCKY PENNY — +3 on this roll.
items.veil_grave: %s's BLACK VEIL swallows the grave. The estate files a complaint.
items.writ: %s unseals a %s WRIT — the die widens for this roll.
items.shovel: %s digs ahead four stones with the PALLBEARER'S SHOVEL. Nothing dares trigger.
items.wisp: A WILL-O’-THE-WISP carries %s up the road.
items.crow_blocked: The crows descend on %s — and find only a BLACK VEIL.
items.bell: THE FUNERAL BELL tolls. %s is dragged back four stones.
items.bell_blocked: THE FUNERAL BELL tolls — but %s walks on beneath a BLACK VEIL.
items.debt_set: %s lays a WREATH OF DEBT upon this stone.
items.debt_paid: %s lands on a WREATH OF DEBT — owed to %s.
items.invitation: %s seals THE INVITATION. The next game will be %s.
items.box_full: %s's hands are full. The box keeps its secrets.

cart.discount_letters: The LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION entitle %s to a mercy discount. It is so noted.
cart.discount_last: The estate notes who trails the wreaths. %s shops at a mercy discount.

awards.announce: Three awards stand announced tonight. The races are public. Run accordingly.
awards.unclaimed: %s — unclaimed. The estate keeps its wreaths.
awards.tie_note: · TIED — the estate’s coin turned, in full view
awards.generous_desc: most pennies spent at the cart

letters.sub: A dignity clause. Mercy prices at the cart, one CROW’S CUT gratis, arrival raised a tier. Tonight only.
reading: Let the record show %s has accepted the LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, pursuant to nothing in particular. The estate extends its cheapest mercy.
The LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION pass to %s — a formality, a kindness, and an insult, in equal measure. So noted.
%s takes up the LETTERS. The estate observes that someone had to.

lastrites.header: LAST RITES — THE CART KEEPS LATE HOURS

finale.opener: The final night is filed. THE READING will now itemise the estate.
finale.stream_arrival: THE READING — ARRIVALS AT THE GATE
finale.stream_liquid: THE READING — LIQUIDATION · 10 PENNIES TO THE WREATH

interlude.header: THE INTERLUDE — THE GROUNDS OFFER ONE MORE GAME
interlude.random_line: The estate deals the night's unplayed invitation: %s.
interlude.doormat_sub: Bottom of the wreaths. The grounds' next game is yours to choose.
interlude.card_line: %s — placements pay the usual pennies and wreaths.

book.selfbet_ribbing: The estate admires confidence, if nothing else.

stirs.omen_intro: The estate has read its own will. It objects.

omen.bone_bridge: The bog is sewing bones. Expect a crossing.
omen.reaper_shortcut: The tall gardener is sharpening. Expect a door.
omen.landslip: The hill is tired of holding. Expect it down.
omen.procession_road: Dead men are pacing a new road. Mind their step.
omen.flood: The garden has been crying again. Bring boots.
omen.hungry_grave: One stone has not eaten. It will.
omen.hearse_moves: The peddler folds his awning. He follows the money.
omen.wake: Mourners are gathering where they are least useful.
omen.crow_court: The crows are convening. Dress accordingly.

fire.bone_bridge: The bog keeps its promise. The bridge is bones. Walk it anyway.
fire.reaper_shortcut:
He has opened a way between the woods and the water.
He did not say for whom.
fire.landslip:
The hill lets go. The woods now end in the valley.
File no complaints.
fire.procession_road:
A road walks itself into the grass.
The dead approve of shortcuts. They took the longest one.
fire.flood:
GARDEN ROW IS CLOSED. Two turns.
The carts have boarded their windows.
fire.hungry_grave:
A stone gives way. The grave beneath was patient.
It is patient no longer.
fire.hearse_moves:
The Peddler's Cart moves on.
Commerce, like grief, relocates.
fire.wake:
A wake convenes on the road. Landing costs a toast.
The rumors are free.
fire.crow_court:
The court is in session.
First past the stone pays the fine.

stirs.crow_strike: The court finds %s guilty of passing. Satisfied.
stirs.wake_toast: %s toasts the departed. The wake repays in rumors.
